1â†’// lifecycle.go implements the generic lifecycle event dispatcher.
     2â†’// It routes normalized events from any agent to the appropriate framework actions.
     3â†’//
     4â†’// The dispatcher inverts the current flow from "agent handler calls framework functions"
     5â†’// to "framework dispatcher calls agent methods." Agents are passive data providers;
     6â†’// the dispatcher handles all orchestration: state transitions, strategy calls,
     7â†’// file change detection, metadata generation.
     8â†’package cli
     9â†’
    10â†’import (
    11â†’	"context"
    12â†’	"errors"
    13â†’	"fmt"
    14â†’	"log/slog"
    15â†’	"os"
    16â†’	"path/filepath"
    17â†’	"strings"
    18â†’	"time"
    19â†’
    20â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    21â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
    22â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    23â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/session"
    24â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/strategy"
    25â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/transcript"
    26â†’)
    27â†’
    28â†’// DispatchLifecycleEvent routes a normalized lifecycle event to the appropriate handler.
    29â†’// Returns nil if the event was handled successfully.
    30â†’func DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag agent.Agent, event *agent.Event) error {
    31â†’	if ag == nil {
    32â†’		return errors.New("agent cannot be nil")
    33â†’	}
    34â†’	if event == nil {
    35â†’		return errors.New("event cannot be nil")
    36â†’	}
    37â†’
    38â†’	switch event.Type {
    39â†’	case agent.SessionStart:
    40â†’		return handleLifecycleSessionStart(ag, event)
    41â†’	case agent.TurnStart:
    42â†’		return handleLifecycleTurnStart(ag, event)
    43â†’	case agent.TurnEnd:
    44â†’		return handleLifecycleTurnEnd(ag, event)
    45â†’	case agent.Compaction:
    46â†’		return handleLifecycleCompaction(ag, event)
    47â†’	case agent.SessionEnd:
    48â†’		return handleLifecycleSessionEnd(ag, event)
    49â†’	case agent.SubagentStart:
    50â†’		return handleLifecycleSubagentStart(ag, event)
    51â†’	case agent.SubagentEnd:
    52â†’		return handleLifecycleSubagentEnd(ag, event)
    53â†’	default:
    54â†’		return fmt.Errorf("unknown lifecycle event type: %d", event.Type)
    55â†’	}
    56â†’}
    57â†’
    58â†’// handleLifecycleSessionStart handles session start: shows banner, checks concurrent sessions,
    59â†’// fires state machine transition.
    60â†’func handleLifecycleSessionStart(ag agent.Agent, event *agent.Event) error {
    61â†’	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "lifecycle"), ag.Name())
    62â†’	logging.Info(logCtx, "session-start",
    63â†’		slog.String("event", event.Type.String()),
    64â†’		slog.String("session_id", event.SessionID),
    65â†’		slog.String("session_ref", event.SessionRef),
    66â†’	)
    67â†’
    68â†’	if event.SessionID == "" {
    69â†’		return fmt.Errorf("no session_id in %s event", event.Type)
    70â†’	}
    71â†’
    72â†’	// Build informational message
    73â†’	message := "\n\nPowered by Entire:\n  This conversation will be linked to your next commit."
    74â†’
    75â†’	// Check for concurrent sessions and append count if any
    76â†’	strat := GetStrategy()
    77â†’	if concurrentChecker, ok := strat.(strategy.ConcurrentSessionChecker); ok {
    78â†’		if count, err := concurrentChecker.CountOtherActiveSessionsWithCheckpoints(event.SessionID); err == nil && count > 0 {
    79â†’			message += fmt.Sprintf("\n  %d other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.\n  Use 'entire status' for more information.", count)
    80â†’		}
    81â†’	}
    82â†’
    83â†’	// Output informational message
    84â†’	if event.ResponseMessage != "" {
    85â†’		message = event.ResponseMessage
    86â†’	}
    87â†’	if err := outputHookResponse(message); err != nil {
    88â†’		return err
    89â†’	}
    90â†’
    91â†’	// Fire EventSessionStart for the current session (if state exists).
    92â†’	if state, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(event.SessionID); loadErr != nil {
    93â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state on start: %v\n", loadErr)
    94â†’	} else if state != nil {
    95â†’		if transErr := strategy.TransitionAndLog(state, session.EventSessionStart, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); transErr != nil {
    96â†’			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: session start transition failed: %v\n", transErr)
    97â†’		}
    98â†’		if saveErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(state); saveErr != nil {
    99â†’			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session state on start: %v\n", saveErr)
   100â†’		}
   101â†’	}
   102â†’
   103â†’	return nil
   104â†’}
   105â†’
   106â†’// handleLifecycleTurnStart handles turn start: captures pre-prompt state,
   107â†’// ensures strategy setup, initializes session.
   108â†’func handleLifecycleTurnStart(ag agent.Agent, event *agent.Event) error {
   109â†’	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "lifecycle"), ag.Name())
   110â†’	logging.Info(logCtx, "turn-start",
   111â†’		slog.String("event", event.Type.String()),
   112â†’		slog.String("session_id", event.SessionID),
   113â†’		slog.String("session_ref", event.SessionRef),
   114â†’	)
   115â†’
   116â†’	sessionID := event.SessionID
   117â†’	if sessionID == "" {
   118â†’		return fmt.Errorf("no session_id in %s event", event.Type)
   119â†’	}
   120â†’
   121â†’	// Capture pre-prompt state (including transcript position via TranscriptAnalyzer)
   122â†’	if err := CapturePrePromptState(ag, sessionID, event.SessionRef); err != nil {
   123â†’		return err
   124â†’	}
   125â†’
   126â†’	// Ensure strategy setup and initialize session
   127â†’	strat := GetStrategy()
   128â†’
   129â†’	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   130â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to ensure strategy setup: %v\n", err)
   131â†’	}
   132â†’
   133â†’	if initializer, ok := strat.(strategy.SessionInitializer); ok {
   134â†’		agentType := ag.Type()
   135â†’		if err := initializer.InitializeSession(sessionID, agentType, event.SessionRef, event.Prompt); err != nil {
   136â†’			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to initialize session state: %v\n", err)
   137â†’		}
   138â†’	}
   139â†’
   140â†’	return nil
   141â†’}
   142â†’
   143â†’// handleLifecycleTurnEnd handles turn end: validates transcript, extracts metadata,
   144â†’// detects file changes, saves step + checkpoint, transitions phase.
   145â†’func handleLifecycleTurnEnd(ag agent.Agent, event *agent.Event) error { //nolint:maintidx // consolidated from two large handlers
   146â†’	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "lifecycle"), ag.Name())
   147â†’	logging.Info(logCtx, "turn-end",
   148â†’		slog.String("event", event.Type.String()),
   149â†’		slog.String("session_id", event.SessionID),
   150â†’		slog.String("session_ref", event.SessionRef),
   151â†’	)
   152â†’
   153â†’	sessionID := event.SessionID
   154â†’	if sessionID == "" {
   155â†’		sessionID = unknownSessionID
   156â†’	}
   157â†’
   158â†’	transcriptRef := event.SessionRef
   159â†’	if transcriptRef == "" || !fileExists(transcriptRef) {
   160â†’		return fmt.Errorf("transcript file not found or empty: %s", transcriptRef)
   161â†’	}
   162â†’
   163â†’	// Early check: bail out quickly if the repo has no commits yet.
   164â†’	if repo, err := strategy.OpenRepository(); err == nil && strategy.IsEmptyRepository(repo) {
   165â†’		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping checkpoint. Will activate after first commit.")

   572â†’// resolveTranscriptOffset determines the transcript offset to use for parsing.
   573â†’// Prefers pre-prompt state, falls back to session state.
   574â†’func resolveTranscriptOffset(preState *PrePromptState, sessionID string) int {
   575â†’	if preState != nil && preState.TranscriptOffset > 0 {
   576â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state found: parsing transcript from offset %d\n", preState.TranscriptOffset)
   577â†’		return preState.TranscriptOffset
   578â†’	}
   579â†’
   580â†’	// Fall back to session state (e.g., auto-commit strategy updates it after each save)
   581â†’	sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   582â†’	if loadErr != nil {
   583â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   584â†’		return 0
   585â†’	}
   586â†’	if sessionState != nil && sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart > 0 {
   587â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Session state found: parsing transcript from offset %d\n", sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   588â†’		return sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart
   589â†’	}
   590â†’
   591â†’	return 0
   592â†’}

   624â†’	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Commit Message: %s\n\n", commitMessage))
   625â†’
   626â†’	if len(prompts) > 0 {
   627â†’		sb.WriteString("## Prompts\n\n")
   628â†’		for i, p := range prompts {
   629â†’			sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("### Prompt %d\n\n%s\n\n", i+1, p))
   630â†’		}
   631â†’	}
   632â†’
   633â†’	if summary != "" {
   634â†’		sb.WriteString("## Summary\n\n")
   635â†’		sb.WriteString(summary)
   636â†’		sb.WriteString("\n")
   637â†’	}
   638â†’
   639â†’	if err := os.WriteFile(contextFile, []byte(sb.String()), 0o600); err != nil {
   640â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write context file: %w", err)
   641â†’	}
   642â†’	return nil
   643â†’}
   644â†’
   645â†’// parseTranscriptForCheckpointUUID is a thin wrapper around transcript parsing for checkpoint UUID lookup.
   646â†’// Returns parsed transcript lines for use with FindCheckpointUUID.
   647â†’func parseTranscriptForCheckpointUUID(transcriptPath string) ([]transcriptLine, int, error) {
   648â†’	lines, total, err := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, 0)
   649â†’	if err != nil {
   650â†’		return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("parsing transcript for checkpoint UUID: %w", err)
   651â†’	}
   652â†’	return lines, total, nil
   653â†’}
   654â†’
   655â†’// transitionSessionTurnEnd transitions the session phase to IDLE and dispatches turn-end actions.
   656â†’func transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID string) {
   657â†’	turnState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   658â†’	if loadErr != nil {
   659â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state for turn end: %v\n", loadErr)
   660â†’		return
   661â†’	}
   662â†’	if turnState == nil {
   663â†’		return
   664â†’	}
   665â†’	if err := strategy.TransitionAndLog(turnState, session.EventTurnEnd, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); err != nil {
   666â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: turn-end transition failed: %v\n", err)
   667â†’	}
   668â†’
   669â†’	// Always dispatch to strategy for turn-end handling. The strategy reads
   670â†’	// work items from state (e.g. TurnCheckpointIDs), not the action list.
   671â†’	strat := GetStrategy()
   672â†’	if handler, ok := strat.(strategy.TurnEndHandler); ok {
   673â†’		if err := handler.HandleTurnEnd(turnState); err != nil {
   674â†’			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: turn-end action dispatch failed: %v\n", err)
   675â†’		}
   676â†’	}
   677â†’
   678â†’	if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(turnState); updateErr != nil {
   679â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session phase on turn end: %v\n", updateErr)
   680â†’	}
   681â†’}
   682â†’
   683â†’// markSessionEnded transitions the session to ENDED phase via the state machine.
   684â†’func markSessionEnded(sessionID string) error {
   685â†’	state, err := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   686â†’	if err != nil {
   687â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session state: %w", err)
   688â†’	}
   689â†’	if state == nil {
   690â†’		return nil // No state file, nothing to update
   691â†’	}
   692â†’
   693â†’	if transErr := strategy.TransitionAndLog(state, session.EventSessionStop, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); transErr != nil {
   694â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: session stop transition failed: %v\n", transErr)
   695â†’	}
   696â†’
   697â†’	now := time.Now()
   698â†’	state.EndedAt = &now
   699â†’
   700â†’	if err := strategy.SaveSessionState(state); err != nil {
   701â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save session state: %w", err)
   702â†’	}
   703â†’	return nil
   704â†’}
   705â†’
   706â†’// logFileChanges logs the files modified, created, and deleted during a session.
   707â†’func logFileChanges(modified, newFiles, deleted []string) {
   708â†’	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files modified during session (%d):\n", len(modified))
   709â†’	for _, file := range modified {
   710â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   711â†’	}
   712â†’	if len(newFiles) > 0 {
   713â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "New files created (%d):\n", len(newFiles))
   714â†’		for _, file := range newFiles {
   715â†’			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   716â†’		}
   717â†’	}
   718â†’	if len(deleted) > 0 {
   719â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files deleted (%d):\n", len(deleted))
   720â†’		for _, file := range deleted {
   721â†’			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   722â†’		}
   723â†’	}
   724â†’}
   725â†’

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"encoding/json"

     6â†’)
     7â†’
     8â†’// EventType represents a normalized lifecycle event from any agent.
     9â†’// Agents translate their native hooks into these event types via ParseHookEvent.
    10â†’type EventType int
    11â†’
    12â†’const (
    13â†’	// SessionStart indicates the agent session has begun.
    14â†’	SessionStart EventType = iota + 1
    15â†’
    16â†’	// TurnStart indicates the user submitted a prompt and the agent is about to work.
    17â†’	TurnStart
    18â†’
    19â†’	// TurnEnd indicates the agent finished responding to a prompt.
    20â†’	TurnEnd
    21â†’
    22â†’	// Compaction indicates the agent is about to compress its context window.
    23â†’	// This triggers the same save logic as TurnEnd but also resets the transcript offset.
    24â†’	Compaction
    25â†’
    26â†’	// SessionEnd indicates the session has been terminated.
    27â†’	SessionEnd
    28â†’
    29â†’	// SubagentStart indicates a subagent (task) has been spawned.
    30â†’	SubagentStart
    31â†’
    32â†’	// SubagentEnd indicates a subagent (task) has completed.
    33â†’	SubagentEnd
    34â†’)
    35â†’
    36â†’// String returns a human-readable name for the event type.
    37â†’func (e EventType) String() string {
    38â†’	switch e {
    39â†’	case SessionStart:
    40â†’		return "SessionStart"
    41â†’	case TurnStart:
    42â†’		return "TurnStart"
    43â†’	case TurnEnd:
    44â†’		return "TurnEnd"
    45â†’	case Compaction:
    46â†’		return "Compaction"
    47â†’	case SessionEnd:
    48â†’		return "SessionEnd"
    49â†’	case SubagentStart:
    50â†’		return "SubagentStart"
    51â†’	case SubagentEnd:
    52â†’		return "SubagentEnd"
    53â†’	default:
    54â†’		return "Unknown"
    55â†’	}
    56â†’}
    57â†’
    58â†’// Event is a normalized lifecycle event produced by an agent's ParseHookEvent method.
    59â†’// The framework dispatcher uses these events to drive checkpoint/session lifecycle actions.
    60â†’type Event struct {
    61â†’	// Type is the kind of lifecycle event.
    62â†’	Type EventType
    63â†’
    64â†’	// SessionID identifies the agent session.

    66â†’
    67â†’	// PreviousSessionID is non-empty when this event represents a session continuation

    69â†’	PreviousSessionID string

    82â†’
    83â†’	// SubagentID identifies the subagent instance (for SubagentEnd events).

1â†’// hook_registry.go provides hook command registration for agents.
     2â†’// The lifecycle dispatcher (DispatchLifecycleEvent) handles all lifecycle events.
     3â†’// PostTodo is the only hook that's handled directly (not via lifecycle dispatcher).
     4â†’package cli
     5â†’
     6â†’import (
     7â†’	"context"
     8â†’	"errors"
     9â†’	"fmt"
    10â†’	"log/slog"
    11â†’	"time"
    12â†’
    13â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    14â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode"
    15â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli"
    16â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
    17â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    18â†’
    19â†’	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    20â†’)
    21â†’
    22â†’// agentHookLogCleanup stores the cleanup function for agent hook logging.
    23â†’// Set by PersistentPreRunE, called by PersistentPostRunE.
    24â†’var agentHookLogCleanup func()
    25â†’
    26â†’// currentHookAgentName stores the agent name for the currently executing hook.
    27â†’// Set by newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging before calling the handler.
    28â†’// This allows handlers to know which agent invoked the hook without guessing.
    29â†’var currentHookAgentName agent.AgentName
    30â†’
    31â†’// GetCurrentHookAgent returns the agent for the currently executing hook.
    32â†’// Returns the agent based on the hook command structure (e.g., "entire hooks claude-code ...")
    33â†’// rather than guessing from directory presence.
    34â†’// Falls back to GetAgent() if not in a hook context.
    35â†’func GetCurrentHookAgent() (agent.Agent, error) {
    36â†’	if currentHookAgentName == "" {
    37â†’		return nil, errors.New("not in a hook context: agent name not set")
    38â†’	}
    39â†’
    40â†’	ag, err := agent.Get(currentHookAgentName)
    41â†’	if err != nil {
    42â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting hook agent %q: %w", currentHookAgentName, err)
    43â†’	}
    44â†’	return ag, nil
    45â†’}
    46â†’
    47â†’// newAgentHooksCmd creates a hooks subcommand for an agent that implements HookHandler.
    48â†’// It dynamically creates subcommands for each hook the agent supports.
    49â†’func newAgentHooksCmd(agentName agent.AgentName, handler agent.HookHandler) *cobra.Command {
    50â†’	cmd := &cobra.Command{
    51â†’		Use:    string(agentName),
    52â†’		Short:  handler.Description() + " hook handlers",
    53â†’		Hidden: true,
    54â†’		PersistentPreRunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    55â†’			agentHookLogCleanup = initHookLogging()
    56â†’			return nil
    57â†’		},
    58â†’		PersistentPostRunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    59â†’			if agentHookLogCleanup != nil {
    60â†’				agentHookLogCleanup()
    61â†’			}
    62â†’			return nil
    63â†’		},
    64â†’	}
    65â†’
    66â†’	for _, hookName := range handler.GetHookNames() {
    67â†’		cmd.AddCommand(newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging(agentName, hookName))
    68â†’	}
    69â†’
    70â†’	return cmd
    71â†’}
    72â†’
    73â†’// getHookType returns the hook type based on the hook name.
    74â†’// Returns "subagent" for task-related hooks (pre-task, post-task, post-todo),
    75â†’// "tool" for tool-related hooks (before-tool, after-tool),
    76â†’// "agent" for all other agent hooks.
    77â†’func getHookType(hookName string) string {
    78â†’	switch hookName {
    79â†’	case claudecode.HookNamePreTask, claudecode.HookNamePostTask, claudecode.HookNamePostTodo:
    80â†’		return "subagent"
    81â†’	case geminicli.HookNameBeforeTool, geminicli.HookNameAfterTool:
    82â†’		return "tool"
    83â†’	default:
    84â†’		return "agent"
    85â†’	}
    86â†’}
    87â†’
    88â†’// newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging creates a command for a specific hook verb with structured logging.
    89â†’// It uses the lifecycle dispatcher (ParseHookEvent â†’ DispatchLifecycleEvent) as the primary path.
    90â†’// PostTodo is handled directly as it's Claude-specific and not part of the lifecycle dispatcher.
    91â†’func newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging(agentName agent.AgentName, hookName string) *cobra.Command {
    92â†’	return &cobra.Command{
    93â†’		Use:    hookName,
    94â†’		Hidden: true,
    95â†’		Short:  "Called on " + hookName,
    96â†’		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    97â†’			// Skip silently if not in a git repository - hooks shouldn't prevent the agent from working
    98â†’			if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
    99â†’				return nil
   100â†’			}
   101â†’
   102â†’			// Skip if Entire is not enabled
   103â†’			enabled, err := IsEnabled()
   104â†’			if err == nil && !enabled {
   105â†’				return nil
   106â†’			}
   107â†’
   108â†’			start := time.Now()
   109â†’
   110â†’			// Initialize logging context with agent name
   111â†’			ctx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), agentName)
   112â†’
   113â†’			// Get strategy name for logging
   114â†’			strategyName := unknownStrategyName //nolint:ineffassign,wastedassign // already present in codebase
   115â†’			strategyName = GetStrategy().Name()
   116â†’
   117â†’			hookType := getHookType(hookName)
   118â†’
   119â†’			logging.Debug(ctx, "hook invoked",
   120â†’				slog.String("hook", hookName),
   121â†’				slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   122â†’				slog.String("strategy", strategyName),
   123â†’			)
   124â†’
   125â†’			// Set the current hook agent so handlers can retrieve it
   126â†’			currentHookAgentName = agentName
   127â†’			defer func() { currentHookAgentName = "" }()
   128â†’
   129â†’			// Use the lifecycle dispatcher for all hooks
   130â†’			var hookErr error
   131â†’			ag, agentErr := agent.Get(agentName)
   132â†’			if agentErr != nil {
   133â†’				return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent %q: %w", agentName, agentErr)
   134â†’			}
   135â†’
   136â†’			// Use cmd.InOrStdin() to support testing with cmd.SetIn()
   137â†’			event, parseErr := ag.ParseHookEvent(hookName, cmd.InOrStdin())
   138â†’			if parseErr != nil {
   139â†’				return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook event: %w", parseErr)
   140â†’			}
   141â†’
   142â†’			if event != nil {
   143â†’				// Lifecycle event â€” use the generic dispatcher
   144â†’				hookErr = DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag, event)
   145â†’			} else if agentName == agent.AgentNameClaudeCode && hookName == claudecode.HookNamePostTodo {
   146â†’				// PostTodo is Claude-specific: creates incremental checkpoints during subagent execution
   147â†’				hookErr = handleClaudeCodePostTodo()
   148â†’			}
   149â†’			// Other pass-through hooks (nil event, no special handling) are no-ops
   150â†’
   151â†’			logging.LogDuration(ctx, slog.LevelDebug, "hook completed", start,
   152â†’				slog.String("hook", hookName),
   153â†’				slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   154â†’				slog.String("strategy", strategyName),
   155â†’				slog.Bool("success", hookErr == nil),
   156â†’			)
   157â†’
   158â†’			return hookErr
   159â†’		},
   160â†’	}
   161â†’}

1â†’// Package agent provides interfaces and types for integrating with coding agents.
     2â†’// It abstracts agent-specific behavior (hooks, log parsing, session storage) so that
     3â†’// the same Strategy implementations can work with any coding agent.
     4â†’package agent
     5â†’
     6â†’import (
     7â†’	"io"
     8â†’)
     9â†’
    10â†’// Agent defines the interface for interacting with a coding agent.
    11â†’// Each agent implementation (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, etc.) converts its
    12â†’// native format to the normalized types defined in this package.
    13â†’//
    14â†’// The interface is organized into four groups:
    15â†’//
    16â†’//   - Identity (5 methods): Name, Type, Description, DetectPresence, ProtectedDirs
    17â†’//   - Event Mapping (2 methods): HookNames, ParseHookEvent
    18â†’//   - Transcript Storage (3 methods): ReadTranscript, ChunkTranscript, ReassembleTranscript
    19â†’//   - Legacy (8 methods): Will be moved to optional interfaces or removed in a future phase
    20â†’type Agent interface {
    21â†’	// --- Identity ---
    22â†’
    23â†’	// Name returns the agent registry key (e.g., "claude-code", "gemini")
    24â†’	Name() AgentName
    25â†’
    26â†’	// Type returns the agent type identifier (e.g., "Claude Code", "Gemini CLI")
    27â†’	// This is stored in metadata and trailers.
    28â†’	Type() AgentType
    29â†’
    30â†’	// Description returns a human-readable description for UI
    31â†’	Description() string
    32â†’
    33â†’	// DetectPresence checks if this agent is configured in the repository
    34â†’	DetectPresence() (bool, error)
    35â†’
    36â†’	// ProtectedDirs returns repo-root-relative directories that should never be

    81â†’	// GetSessionDir returns where agent stores session data for this repo.

    87â†’	// ReadSession reads session data from agent's storage.

    92â†’

    96â†’
    97â†’// HookSupport is implemented by agents with lifecycle hooks.
    98â†’// This optional interface allows agents like Claude Code and Cursor to
    99â†’// install and manage hooks that notify Entire of agent events.
   100â†’type HookSupport interface {

   102â†’

   114â†’

   117â†’}
   118â†’
   119â†’// HookHandler is implemented by agents that define their own hook vocabulary.
   120â†’// HookNames() is now part of the core Agent interface.
   121â†’// This interface is kept for backward compatibility during migration.
   122â†’type HookHandler interface {
   123â†’	Agent
   124â†’

   126â†’	GetHookNames() []string
   127â†’}
   128â†’
   129â†’// FileWatcher is implemented by agents that use file-based detection.
   130â†’// Agents like Aider that don't support hooks can use file watching
   131â†’// to detect session activity.
   132â†’type FileWatcher interface {
   133â†’	Agent
   134â†’

   136â†’	GetWatchPaths() ([]string, error)
   137â†’

   139â†’	OnFileChange(path string) (*SessionChange, error)

   141â†’
   142â†’// TranscriptAnalyzer provides format-specific transcript parsing.
   143â†’// Agents that implement this get richer checkpoints (transcript-derived file lists,
   144â†’// prompts, summaries). Agents that don't still participate in the checkpoint lifecycle
   145â†’// via git-status-based file detection and raw transcript storage.
   146â†’type TranscriptAnalyzer interface {
   147â†’	Agent
   148â†’
   149â†’	// GetTranscriptPosition returns the current position (length) of a transcript.
   150â†’	// For JSONL formats (Claude Code), this is the line count.
   151â†’	// For JSON formats (Gemini CLI), this is the message count.
   152â†’	// Returns 0 if the file doesn't exist or is empty.
   153â†’	GetTranscriptPosition(path string) (int, error)
   154â†’
   155â†’	// ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset extracts files modified since a given offset.
   156â†’	// For JSONL formats (Claude Code), offset is the starting line number.
   157â†’	// For JSON formats (Gemini CLI), offset is the starting message index.

   162â†’	ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset(path string, startOffset int) (files []string, currentPosition int, err error)

   165â†’	ExtractPrompts(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) ([]string, error)
   166â†’
   167â†’	// ExtractSummary extracts a summary of the session from the transcript.
   168â†’	ExtractSummary(sessionRef string) (string, error)
   169â†’}
   170â†’
   171â†’// TranscriptPreparer is called before ReadTranscript to handle agent-specific
   172â†’// flush/sync requirements (e.g., Claude Code's async transcript writing).
   173â†’// The framework calls PrepareTranscript before ReadTranscript if implemented.
   174â†’type TranscriptPreparer interface {
   175â†’	Agent
   176â†’

   178â†’	// For Claude Code, this waits for the async transcript flush to complete.

   180â†’}
   181â†’
   182â†’// TokenCalculator provides token usage calculation for a session.
   183â†’// The framework calls this during step save and checkpoint if implemented.
   184â†’type TokenCalculator interface {

   188â†’	CalculateTokenUsage(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) (*TokenUsage, error)
   189â†’}

   311â†’
   312â†’// Strategy defines the interface for git operation strategies.

   316â†’// by the CLI layer, not the strategy. The strategy receives pre-computed

   329â†’	// SaveStep is called on Stop to save all session changes

   332â†’
   333â†’	// SaveTaskStep is called by PostToolUse[Task] hook when a subagent completes.
   334â†’	// Creates a checkpoint commit with task metadata for later rewind.
   335â†’	// Different strategies may handle this differently:
   336â†’	// - Commit strategy: commits to active branch

    12â†’// TestOldIdleSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated verifies that when an old IDLE session
    13â†’// exists and a new ACTIVE session makes a commit, the old IDLE session's BaseCommit
    14â†’// is NOT updated to the new HEAD.
    15â†’//
    16â†’// This is a regression test for the bug where old sessions (IDLE/ENDED) would
    17â†’// have their BaseCommit incorrectly updated, causing them to be condensed on
    18â†’// future commits because their BaseCommit matched the new shadow branch.
    19â†’//
    20â†’// Scenario:
    21â†’// 1. Create an old session (session1), run full workflow, set to IDLE
    22â†’// 2. Make an unrelated commit to move HEAD forward (simulating time passing)
    23â†’// 3. Create a new session (session2), make changes
    24â†’// 4. Commit from session2
    25â†’// 5. Verify session1's BaseCommit was NOT updated
    26â†’func TestOldIdleSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated(t *testing.T) {
    27â†’	t.Parallel()
    28â†’	env := NewTestEnv(t)
    29â†’	defer env.Cleanup()
    30â†’
    31â†’	// ========================================
    32â†’	// Setup
    33â†’	// ========================================
    34â†’	env.InitRepo()
    35â†’	env.WriteFile("README.md", "# Test Repository")
    36â†’	env.GitAdd("README.md")
    37â†’	env.GitCommit("Initial commit")
    38â†’	env.GitCheckoutNewBranch("feature/test-base-commit")
    39â†’	env.InitEntire(strategy.StrategyNameManualCommit)
    40â†’
    41â†’	// ========================================
    42â†’	// Phase 1: Create first session (will become IDLE)
    43â†’	// ========================================
    44â†’	t.Log("Phase 1: Creating first session that will become IDLE")
    45â†’
    46â†’	session1 := env.NewSession()
    47â†’	if err := env.SimulateUserPromptSubmit(session1.ID); err != nil {
    48â†’		t.Fatalf("SimulateUserPromptSubmit for session1 failed: %v", err)
    49â†’	}
    50â†’
    51â†’	// Create a file and checkpoint
    52â†’	env.WriteFile("file1.txt", "content from session 1")
    53â†’	session1.CreateTranscript(
    54â†’		"Create file1",
    55â†’		[]FileChange{{Path: "file1.txt", Content: "content from session 1"}},
    56â†’	)
    57â†’	if err := env.SimulateStop(session1.ID, session1.TranscriptPath); err != nil {
    58â†’		t.Fatalf("SimulateStop for session1 failed: %v", err)
    59â†’	}
    60â†’
    61â†’	// Verify session1 is IDLE
    62â†’	state1, err := env.GetSessionState(session1.ID)
    63â†’	if err != nil {
    64â†’		t.Fatalf("GetSessionState for session1 failed: %v", err)
    65â†’	}
    66â†’	if state1.Phase != session.PhaseIdle {
    67â†’		t.Fatalf("Expected session1 to be IDLE, got %s", state1.Phase)
    68â†’	}
    69â†’
    70â†’	// Record session1's BaseCommit BEFORE the unrelated commit
    71â†’	session1OriginalBaseCommit := state1.BaseCommit
    72â†’	t.Logf("Session1 original BaseCommit: %s", session1OriginalBaseCommit[:7])
    73â†’
    74â†’	// ========================================
    75â†’	// Phase 2: Make an unrelated commit to move HEAD forward
    76â†’	// ========================================
    77â†’	t.Log("Phase 2: Making unrelated commit to move HEAD forward")
    78â†’
    79â†’	env.WriteFile("unrelated.txt", "unrelated file")
    80â†’	env.GitAdd("unrelated.txt")
    81â†’	env.GitCommit("Unrelated commit without checkpoint trailer")
    82â†’
    83â†’	newHeadAfterUnrelated := env.GetHeadHash()
    84â†’	t.Logf("HEAD after unrelated commit: %s", newHeadAfterUnrelated[:7])
    85â†’
    86â†’	// ========================================
    87â†’	// Phase 3: Create second session (ACTIVE)
    88â†’	// ========================================
    89â†’	t.Log("Phase 3: Creating second session")
    90â†’
    91â†’	session2 := env.NewSession()
    92â†’	if err := env.SimulateUserPromptSubmit(session2.ID); err != nil {
    93â†’		t.Fatalf("SimulateUserPromptSubmit for session2 failed: %v", err)
    94â†’	}
    95â†’
    96â†’	// Create a file and checkpoint for session2
    97â†’	env.WriteFile("file2.txt", "content from session 2")
    98â†’	session2.CreateTranscript(
    99â†’		"Create file2",
   100â†’		[]FileChange{{Path: "file2.txt", Content: "content from session 2"}},
   101â†’	)
   102â†’	if err := env.SimulateStop(session2.ID, session2.TranscriptPath); err != nil {
   103â†’		t.Fatalf("SimulateStop for session2 failed: %v", err)
   104â†’	}
   105â†’
   106â†’	// Set session2 to ACTIVE (simulating agent mid-turn)
   107â†’	state2, err := env.GetSessionState(session2.ID)
   108â†’	if err != nil {
   109â†’		t.Fatalf("GetSessionState for session2 failed: %v", err)
   110â†’	}
   111â†’	state2.Phase = session.PhaseActive
   112â†’	if err := env.WriteSessionState(session2.ID, state2); err != nil {
   113â†’		t.Fatalf("WriteSessionState for session2 failed: %v", err)
   114â†’	}
   115â†’
   116â†’	// ========================================
   117â†’	// Phase 4: Commit from session2 (triggers PostCommit)
   118â†’	// ========================================
   119â†’	t.Log("Phase 4: Committing from session2")
   120â†’
   121â†’	env.GitAdd("file2.txt")
   122â†’	env.GitCommitWithShadowHooks("Commit from session2", "file2.txt")
   123â†’
   124â†’	finalHead := env.GetHeadHash()
   125â†’	t.Logf("Final HEAD after session2 commit: %s", finalHead[:7])
   126â†’
   127â†’	// ========================================
   128â†’	// Phase 5: Verify session1's BaseCommit was NOT updated
   129â†’	// ========================================
   130â†’	t.Log("Phase 5: Verifying session1's BaseCommit was NOT updated")
   131â†’
   132â†’	state1After, err := env.GetSessionState(session1.ID)
   133â†’	if err != nil {
   134â†’		t.Fatalf("GetSessionState for session1 after commit failed: %v", err)
   135â†’	}
   136â†’
   137â†’	if state1After.BaseCommit != session1OriginalBaseCommit {
   138â†’		t.Errorf("OLD IDLE session's BaseCommit was incorrectly updated!\n"+
   139â†’			"Expected: %s (original)\n"+
   140â†’			"Got:      %s (new HEAD)",
   141â†’			session1OriginalBaseCommit[:7], state1After.BaseCommit[:7])
   142â†’	}
   143â†’
   144â†’	if state1After.BaseCommit == finalHead {
   145â†’		t.Errorf("OLD IDLE session's BaseCommit incorrectly matches new HEAD: %s", finalHead[:7])
   146â†’	}
   147â†’
   148â†’	// Session2's BaseCommit SHOULD be updated (it was the active session)
   149â†’	state2After, err := env.GetSessionState(session2.ID)
   150â†’	if err != nil {
   151â†’		t.Fatalf("GetSessionState for session2 after commit failed: %v", err)
   152â†’	}
   153â†’	if state2After.BaseCommit != finalHead {
   154â†’		t.Errorf("NEW ACTIVE session's BaseCommit should be updated to new HEAD\n"+
   155â†’			"Expected: %s\n"+
   156â†’			"Got:      %s",
   157â†’			finalHead[:7], state2After.BaseCommit[:7])
   158â†’	}
   159â†’
   160â†’	t.Log("Test completed")
   161â†’}

   163â†’// TestOldEndedSession_BaseCommitNotUpdated verifies that when an old ENDED session
   164â†’// exists and a new ACTIVE session makes a commit, the old ENDED session's BaseCommit

   168â†’	env := NewTestEnv(t)

   183â†’	// ========================================

   185â†’
   186â†’	session1 := env.NewSession()

   195â†’		[]FileChange{{Path: "file1.txt", Content: "content from session 1"}},

   197â†’	if err := env.SimulateStop(session1.ID, session1.TranscriptPath); err != nil {
   198â†’		t.Fatalf("SimulateStop for session1 failed: %v", err)
   199â†’	}

   239â†’	// Create a file and checkpoint for session2
   240â†’	env.WriteFile("file2.txt", "content from session 2")
   241â†’	session2.CreateTranscript(
   242â†’		"Create file2",
   243â†’		[]FileChange{{Path: "file2.txt", Content: "content from session 2"}},
   244â†’	)

   251â†’	if err != nil {
   252â†’		t.Fatalf("GetSessionState for session2 failed: %v", err)
   253â†’	}

   255â†’	if err := env.WriteSessionState(session2.ID, state2); err != nil {
   256â†’		t.Fatalf("WriteSessionState for session2 failed: %v", err)

1â†’// hooks_claudecode_posttodo.go contains the PostTodo hook handler for Claude Code.
     2â†’// This is a Claude-specific hook that creates incremental checkpoints during subagent execution.
     3â†’// It's not part of the generic lifecycle dispatcher because it requires special handling:
     4â†’// - Only fires for TodoWrite tool invocations
     5â†’// - Creates incremental checkpoints (not full checkpoints)
     6â†’// - Only activates when in subagent context (pre-task file exists)
     7â†’package cli
     8â†’
     9â†’import (
    10â†’	"context"
    11â†’	"fmt"
    12â†’	"log/slog"
    13â†’	"os"
    14â†’
    15â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    16â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
    17â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    18â†’	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/strategy"
    19â†’)
    20â†’
    21â†’// handleClaudeCodePostTodo handles the PostToolUse[TodoWrite] hook for subagent checkpoints.
    22â†’// Creates a checkpoint if we're in a subagent context (active pre-task file exists).
    23â†’// Skips silently if not in subagent context (main agent).
    24â†’func handleClaudeCodePostTodo() error {
    25â†’	input, err := parseSubagentCheckpointHookInput(os.Stdin)
    26â†’	if err != nil {
    27â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PostToolUse[TodoWrite] input: %w", err)
    28â†’	}
    29â†’
    30â†’	// Get agent for logging context
    31â†’	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
    32â†’	if err != nil {
    33â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
    34â†’	}
    35â†’
    36â†’	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
    37â†’	logging.Info(logCtx, "post-todo",
    38â†’		slog.String("hook", "post-todo"),
    39â†’		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
    40â†’		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
    41â†’		slog.String("transcript_path", input.TranscriptPath),
    42â†’		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
    43â†’	)
    44â†’
    45â†’	// Check if we're in a subagent context by looking for an active pre-task file
    46â†’	taskToolUseID, found := FindActivePreTaskFile()
    47â†’	if !found {
    48â†’		// Not in subagent context - this is a main agent TodoWrite, skip
    49â†’		return nil
    50â†’	}
    51â†’
    52â†’	// Skip on default branch to avoid polluting main/master history
    53â†’	if skip, branchName := ShouldSkipOnDefaultBranch(); skip {
    54â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping incremental checkpoint on branch '%s'\n", branchName)
    55â†’		return nil
    56â†’	}
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Detect file changes since last checkpoint
    59â†’	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(nil)
    60â†’	if err != nil {
    61â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to detect changed files: %v\n", err)
    62â†’		return nil
    63â†’	}
    64â†’
    65â†’	// If no file changes, skip creating a checkpoint
    66â†’	if len(changes.Modified) == 0 && len(changes.New) == 0 && len(changes.Deleted) == 0 {
    67â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping incremental checkpoint\n")
    68â†’		return nil
    69â†’	}
    70â†’
    71â†’	// Get git author
    72â†’	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
    73â†’	if err != nil {
    74â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get git author: %v\n", err)
    75â†’		return nil
    76â†’	}
    77â†’
    78â†’	// Get the active strategy
    79â†’	strat := GetStrategy()
    80â†’
    81â†’	// Get the session ID from the transcript path or input, then transform to Entire session ID
    82â†’	sessionID := input.SessionID
    83â†’	if sessionID == "" {
    84â†’		sessionID = paths.ExtractSessionIDFromTranscriptPath(input.TranscriptPath)
    85â†’	}
    86â†’
    87â†’	// Get next checkpoint sequence
    88â†’	seq := GetNextCheckpointSequence(sessionID, taskToolUseID)
    89â†’
    90â†’	// Extract the todo content from the tool_input.
    91â†’	// PostToolUse receives the NEW todo list where the just-completed work is
    92â†’	// marked as "completed". The last completed item is the work that was just done.
    93â†’	todoContent := ExtractLastCompletedTodoFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
    94â†’	if todoContent == "" {
    95â†’		// No completed items - this is likely the first TodoWrite (planning phase).
    96â†’		// Check if there are any todos at all to avoid duplicate messages.
    97â†’		todoCount := CountTodosFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
    98â†’		if todoCount > 0 {
    99â†’			// Use "Planning: N todos" format for the first TodoWrite
   100â†’			todoContent = fmt.Sprintf("Planning: %d todos", todoCount)
   101â†’		}
   102â†’		// If todoCount == 0, todoContent remains empty and FormatIncrementalMessage
   103â†’		// will fall back to "Checkpoint #N" format
   104â†’	}
   105â†’
   106â†’	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   107â†’	var agentType agent.AgentType
   108â†’	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   109â†’		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   110â†’	}
   111â†’
   112â†’	// Build incremental task step context
   113â†’	ctx := strategy.TaskStepContext{
   114â†’		SessionID:           sessionID,
   115â†’		ToolUseID:           taskToolUseID,
   116â†’		ModifiedFiles:       changes.Modified,
   117â†’		NewFiles:            changes.New,
   118â†’		DeletedFiles:        changes.Deleted,
   119â†’		TranscriptPath:      input.TranscriptPath,
   120â†’		AuthorName:          author.Name,
   121â†’		AuthorEmail:         author.Email,
   122â†’		IsIncremental:       true,
   123â†’		IncrementalSequence: seq,
   124â†’		IncrementalType:     input.ToolName,
   125â†’		IncrementalData:     input.ToolInput,
   126â†’		TodoContent:         todoContent,
   127â†’		AgentType:           agentType,
   128â†’	}
   129â†’
   130â†’	// Save incremental task step
   131â†’	if err := strat.SaveTaskStep(ctx); err != nil {
   132â†’		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to save incremental task step: %v\n", err)
   133â†’		return nil
   134â†’	}
   135â†’
   136â†’	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] Created incremental checkpoint #%d for %s (task: %s)\n",
   137â†’		seq, input.ToolName, taskToolUseID[:min(12, len(taskToolUseID))])
   138â†’	return nil
   139â†’}
   140â†’

1â†’# Plan: Evolve Agent Interface for Contributor-Ready Plugin Surface
     2â†’
     3â†’## Context
     4â†’
     5â†’The CLI is now open source and receiving contributions. The current agent integration surface area requires contributors to:
     6â†’1. Implement a 17-method `Agent` interface with unclear required-vs-optional boundaries
     7â†’2. Write per-agent handler functions in the `cli` package (due to import cycles)
     8â†’3. Register handlers via stringly-typed `(agentName, hookName)` pairs in `hook_registry.go`
     9â†’4. Understand implicit mappings from agent-native hooks to lifecycle semantics
    10â†’5. Duplicate framework logic (state transitions, strategy calls, file detection) in each handler
    11â†’
    12â†’This plan redesigns the agent interface around explicit lifecycle events with a generic framework dispatcher, making it compile-time safe and contributor-friendly.
    13â†’
    14â†’## Design Rationale
    15â†’
    16â†’### The core insight: agents are data providers, not orchestrators
    17â†’
    18â†’The current architecture grew organically as we added agents. Each agent's handler file (`hooks_claudecode_handlers.go`, `hooks_geminicli_handlers.go`) contains a mix of agent-specific parsing and framework orchestration logic â€” state transitions, strategy calls, file change detection, context generation. When a contributor wants to add a new agent, they have to understand and reproduce all of that framework machinery, which is both error-prone and creates maintenance burden as the framework evolves.
    19â†’
    20â†’The key realization is that agents should be **passive data providers**. They know how to parse their own hook inputs and transcript formats, but they should never drive the checkpoint/step lifecycle themselves. The framework should call *into* the agent for data (transcript bytes, modified file lists, prompts, summaries) and handle all the orchestration â€” state machine transitions, strategy method calls, file change detection, metadata generation â€” in one place.
    21â†’
    22â†’This inverts the current flow from "agent handler calls framework functions" to "framework dispatcher calls agent methods."
    23â†’
    24â†’### Separating Events from Actions
    25â†’
    26â†’We initially conflated what happens (events) with what the framework does about it (actions). For example, "StepSave" was initially proposed as an event type, but it's actually a framework *response* to a TurnEnd event. Getting this distinction right was critical:
    27â†’
    28â†’- **Events** are inputs â€” things that happen in the outside world. They come from agents (SessionStart, TurnStart, TurnEnd, Compaction, SessionEnd, SubagentStart, SubagentEnd) or from git (GitCommit, PrePush). An agent's only job is to translate its native hooks into these normalized events.
    29â†’- **Actions** are outputs â€” what the framework does in response. StepSave, Checkpoint, Condense, PhaseTransition, ResetTranscriptOffset. These are entirely internal to the framework and invisible to agent implementers.
    30â†’
    31â†’This means a contributor adding a new agent only needs to think about: "which of my hooks correspond to which lifecycle events?" They never need to understand the action dispatch machinery.
    32â†’
    33â†’### ParseHookEvent as the contribution surface
    34â†’
    35â†’The most important method in the new interface is `ParseHookEvent(hookName string, stdin io.Reader) (*Event, error)`. This is where 90% of agent-specific work lives. The agent reads its native hook payload from stdin, extracts the session ID, transcript path, and any other relevant data, and returns a normalized `Event` â€” or `nil` if the hook has no lifecycle significance.
    36â†’
    37â†’This design handles the "pass-through hook" problem elegantly. Agents like Gemini have hooks (before-tool, after-tool, before-model, etc.) that we acknowledge but don't act on. Rather than requiring dummy handler registrations for each, the agent simply returns `nil` from `ParseHookEvent` and the dispatcher does nothing.
    38â†’
    39â†’### Why transcript operations are core, not optional
    40â†’
    41â†’We initially considered making transcript reading/chunking/analysis optional interfaces, since not all hypothetical future agents might have accessible transcripts. But transcript data is fundamental to the product â€” it's what gets stored in checkpoints, what drives the "files modified" detection, and what enables session logs and summaries. An agent that can't provide transcript access can't meaningfully participate in the checkpoint lifecycle. Making these methods core forces contributors to think about transcript access upfront rather than discovering the gap later.
    42â†’
    43â†’### Step vs Checkpoint terminology
    44â†’
    45â†’The codebase had been using "checkpoint" loosely for two distinct concepts: the temporary per-turn saves on shadow branches and the permanent persisted saves on `entire/checkpoints/v1`. We established precise terminology:
    46â†’- A **Step** is saved to the shadow branch â€” it's temporary, per-turn, and gets cleaned up on condensation
    47â†’- A **Checkpoint** is persisted to `entire/checkpoints/v1` â€” it's permanent and survives branch operations
    48â†’
    49â†’This distinction matters because both happen on TurnEnd (the framework saves a step *and* writes a checkpoint), but they have different lifecycles and storage mechanisms. The strategy interface rename (`SaveChanges` â†’ `SaveStep`, `SaveTaskCheckpoint` â†’ `SaveTaskStep`) makes the code self-documenting.
    50â†’
    51â†’### Compaction and session handoff
    52â†’
    53â†’Two lifecycle events we identified during design that weren't previously modeled:
    54â†’
    55â†’**Compaction** occurs when an agent compresses its context window (e.g., Gemini's pre-compress hook). This triggers the same save logic as TurnEnd but also resets the transcript offset, since the transcript may be truncated or reorganized. We also added defensive detection: if `GetTranscriptPosition()` returns a value smaller than the stored offset, the framework treats it as an implicit compaction even without an explicit event.
    56â†’
    57â†’**Session handoff** occurs when Claude starts a new session ID (observed after exiting plan mode). Rather than building complex session-linking machinery now, we added a `PreviousSessionID` field on `Event` for future use. The current shadow branch architecture already handles this gracefully â€” multiple session IDs naturally share the same shadow branch and condense together.
    58â†’
    59â†’### Terminology (established in this plan)
    60â†’- **Step** = saved to shadow branch (`entire/<hash>-<worktreeHash>`) â€” temporary, per-turn
    61â†’- **Checkpoint** = persisted to `entire/checkpoints/v1` â€” permanent, on turn end and git commit
    62â†’
    63â†’### Prerequisites
    64â†’- **ENT-250** (ActionHandler interface for compile-time exhaustive action dispatch) should be completed first. It's self-contained and establishes the pattern we'll extend here.
    65â†’
    66â†’---
    67â†’
    68â†’## Phase 1: Define Event Types and New Agent Interface
    69â†’
    70â†’### 1a. Event types (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/event.go` â€” new file)
    71â†’
    72â†’```go
    73â†’type EventType int
    74â†’const (
    75â†’    SessionStart  EventType = iota  // Agent session begins
    76â†’    TurnStart                       // User submitted a prompt
    77â†’    TurnEnd                         // Agent finished responding
    78â†’    Compaction                      // Agent about to compress context
    79â†’    SessionEnd                      // Session terminated
    80â†’    SubagentStart                   // Subagent spawned
    81â†’    SubagentEnd                     // Subagent completed
    82â†’)
    83â†’
    84â†’type Event struct {
    85â†’    Type              EventType
    86â†’    SessionID         string
    87â†’    PreviousSessionID string     // Non-empty = continuation/handoff
    88â†’    SessionRef        string     // Path to transcript file
    89â†’    Prompt            string     // For TurnStart: user's prompt
    90â†’    Timestamp         time.Time
    91â†’
    92â†’    // Subagent-specific
    93â†’    ToolUseID         string
    94â†’    SubagentID        string
    95â†’    ToolInput         json.RawMessage  // Raw tool input (for subagent type/description extraction)
    96â†’
    97â†’    // Response â€” message to show the user via the agent
    98â†’    ResponseMessage   string
    99â†’}
   100â†’```
   101â†’
   102â†’### 1b. New `Agent` interface (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/agent.go` â€” modify)
   103â†’
   104â†’Replace the current 17-method interface with a 14-method interface in three clear groups:
   105â†’
   106â†’```go
   107â†’type Agent interface {
   108â†’    // --- Identity (5 methods, all trivial one-liners) ---
   109â†’    Name() AgentName
   110â†’    Type() AgentType

   120â†’    ReadTranscript(sessionRef string) ([]byte, error)
